# ImgSplit — Complete Reference

> Free online image splitter — split, crop, and grid-divide images with pixel-perfect precision. 100% browser-based, no upload needed, privacy-first.

## What is ImgSplit?

ImgSplit is a free, open-source online image splitting tool that runs entirely in the browser. It allows users to divide images into custom segments using drag-and-drop split lines, create social media grids, and download results as ZIP files — all without uploading images to any server.

ImgSplit uses the HTML5 Canvas API for all image processing, ensuring complete privacy and zero-latency operations. No account is required, and the tool works on desktop and mobile browsers.

## Who Built ImgSplit?

ImgSplit was created by ZooHero, a full-stack developer focused on building free, privacy-first web tools.

- Website: https://imgsplit.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/ZooHero500/online-image-segmentation
- Twitter: https://x.com/zooheroes

## Core Features

- **Drag-and-drop split lines**: Add up to 20 horizontal and 20 vertical split lines, drag them to exact positions
- **Snap alignment**: Lines snap to common positions for precise, even splits
- **Grid splitting**: Pre-configured grid layouts (3×3, 1×3, 2×2) for social media posts
- **AI-powered segmentation**: Automatically detect and segment image elements
- **Pixel-perfect precision**: Crops at original resolution with zero quality loss — no compression, no scaling
- **Multi-format support**: PNG, JPG/JPEG, and WebP, up to 20MB per file
- **One-click ZIP download**: Download all split pieces as a ZIP archive, or save individually
- **Live preview**: See split results in real-time as you adjust line positions
- **Cross-device**: Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile browsers
- **Privacy-first**: All processing in-browser, images never uploaded to any server

## Tools

### Image Splitter (Main Tool)
- URL: https://imgsplit.com
- Full-featured image splitter with custom horizontal and vertical split lines
- Supports drag-and-drop upload, snap alignment, and batch processing
- Use for: general image splitting, custom crop regions, batch operations

### Grid Splitter
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/grid
- Split images into pre-configured grid tiles for social media
- Grid types: 3×3 (nine-grid for Instagram profiles), 1×3 (horizontal triptych), 2×2 (four-panel)
- Features: drag to reposition within grid frame, optional white borders, zoom control
- Use for: Instagram grid posts, Facebook multi-image posts, social media profiles

### Split in Half
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-in-half
- Divide images into two equal parts, horizontally or vertically
- Use for: before/after comparisons, simple two-part layouts

### Split Horizontally
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-horizontally
- Cut images into horizontal rows (top-to-bottom slices)
- Use for: website banners, email header slicing, horizontal layouts

### Split Vertically
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-vertically
- Cut images into vertical columns (left-to-right slices)
- Use for: side-by-side comparisons, vertical panel layouts

### Equal Parts Splitter
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-into-equal-parts
- Divide images into any number of equal segments
- Use for: uniform grids, tile creation, pattern slicing

### Long Screenshot Splitter
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-long-screenshot
- Break tall screenshots into page-sized segments
- Use for: scrolling screenshots, long chat logs, tall infographics

### Instagram Splitter
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-for-instagram
- Optimized for Instagram's 1080×1080px format
- Grid types for profile grids and carousel posts
- Use for: Instagram profile mosaics, carousel content

### Social Media Grid Splitter
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-for-wechat
- Create grid posts optimized for social media platforms
- Use for: WeChat moments, Facebook posts, Twitter/X media grids

### Print Splitter
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/split-for-print
- Tile large images across multiple print pages
- Use for: posters, banners, large-format printing

### No-Photoshop Slicer
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/no-photoshop-slicer
- Free alternative to Photoshop's slice tool
- Use for: web design asset export, UI slicing without Photoshop

### All Tools Directory
- URL: https://imgsplit.com/tools
- Browse all image splitting tools with descriptions

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What image formats does ImgSplit support?
ImgSplit supports PNG, JPG/JPEG, and WebP images, with a maximum file size of 20MB per image.

### Does splitting reduce image quality?
No. ImgSplit crops at the original resolution using the Canvas API — no compression, no scaling, no quality loss.

### Is my image data safe?
Yes. All processing happens locally in your browser using the HTML5 Canvas API. No image data is ever uploaded to any server. Everything is cleared when you close the page.

### How many split lines can I add?
Up to 20 horizontal and 20 vertical split lines per image, allowing for very fine grid splits.

### Do I need to create an account?
No. No registration, no login required. Open the page and start using it — completely free.

### Does it work on mobile?
Yes. ImgSplit is fully responsive and works on phones, tablets, and desktop browsers.

### Is ImgSplit open source?
Yes. The full source code is available at https://github.com/ZooHero500/online-image-segmentation under the MIT License.

## Technical Architecture

- **Framework**: Next.js 16 with App Router
- **Image Processing**: HTML5 Canvas API (client-side only)
- **Languages**: Available in English, Chinese (zh-CN), Japanese (ja), Korean (ko), and Spanish (es)
- **Hosting**: Vercel
- **License**: MIT (open source)

## Key Facts

- Price: Free (no signup required, no premium tiers)
- Privacy: All processing in-browser — no images uploaded, no server-side processing
- Limits: Up to 20 horizontal + 20 vertical split lines per image
- File size: Up to 20MB per image
- Formats: PNG, JPG/JPEG, WebP
- Open source: https://github.com/ZooHero500/online-image-segmentation
- Contact: zoohero.dev@gmail.com
